key_insight: A gradient Al2O3/Cr2O3 coating made by detonation spraying outperformed a bilayer design by reducing defects and improving hardness, wear, and corrosion resistance.
engineering_relevance: Graded coating architecture can be used to extend the service life of stainless-steel components exposed to friction and saline corrosion.
method: Detonation-sprayed Al2O3/Cr2O3 coatings on 316L stainless steel
Compared gradient vs bilayer microstructure and phase state with XRD, SEM, and EDS
Evaluated hardness, scratch resistance, tribology, and corrosion in 3.5% NaCl
evidence_signal: The gradient coating showed lower porosity (0.934% vs 1.285%), higher hardness (462 vs 401 HV), lower friction (0.52–0.55 vs 0.58–0.60), and lower corrosion current density (0.24155 vs 0.50540 µA/cm2).
watch_item: The improvements are demonstrated at laboratory scale, so durability under long-term thermal cycling, impact loading, or service-environment aging still needs validation.
paper_title: Structural and Phase State and Properties of Gradient and Bilayer Al2O3/Cr2O3 Coatings Obtained by Detonation Spraying
